title: CAR (codex-autorunner) Architecture Tour
description: A guided walkthrough of CAR, the agent coordination hub that drives coding agents via markdown tickets as the control plane.

steps.title: Welcome to CAR
steps.description: CAR is a meta-harness for coding agents. It coordinates agents like Codex and OpenCode using markdown tickets as the control plane. The CLI (cli.py) and web server (server.py) are the two main entry points. The car script at the repo root bootstraps the environment.

steps.title: Core Engine
steps.description: The protocol-agnostic control layer that manages config, state (SQLite), the runner loop, prompt building, locks, and stop rules. The hub module supervises multiple repos and worktrees. The PMA (Project Manager Agent) subsystem handles conversational project management with its own queue, dispatch, lifecycle, and safety modules.

steps.title: Tickets: The Control Plane
steps.description: Tickets are markdown files with frontmatter that drive the entire system. This package handles parsing, state management, linting, bulk operations, and the ticket runner that picks the next incomplete ticket. Agents read and write tickets to coordinate work. Tickets are code.

steps.title: Agent Adapters
steps.description: CAR supports multiple coding agents through a clean adapter pattern. base.py defines the interface, and each agent (Codex, OpenCode) has its own package with a harness that implements the agent lifecycle. The integrations/agents layer handles backend orchestration and runtime wiring, keeping the engine vendor-neutral.

steps.title: Integrations & Surfaces
steps.description: Platform integrations for user interaction. The chat package provides a platform-agnostic abstraction implemented by Telegram and Discord adapters. GitHub handles issue-to-spec ingestion and PR sync. Docker manages container-based execution. The web package powers the FastAPI UI with PTY terminal sessions, and the voice package provides Whisper-based transcription.

steps.title: The Full Picture
steps.description: CAR follows a clean layered design: the protocol-agnostic core engine at the center, agent adapters and ticket management as the execution layer, platform integrations for user interaction, and a web UI plus CLI as the primary control planes. The filesystem is the first-class data plane, tickets are code, and agents are the execution layer.
